findings.problem: Render half of the compute-reachability P1 is still open: the surfacing half IS closed at 98726ca (summary_for_llm(include_results=True) digest at analysis_notebook.py:227-276, caller outline_agent.py:1174; _compute_result_check appends workspace.compute_results at :1609 with renderable=False; carried to exit as notebook_compute at :2108; inherited tests 14/14 pass). But no tool bridges the agent loop to the verified [#calc:] lane: quantified_analysis.execute_quantified_model (generator/quantified_analysis.py:650), QuantifiedResult.calc_token (:617) and bind_calc_tokens (:782) plus tradeoff_modeler's ModelSpec/whitelisted-AST validator (synthesis/tradeoff_modeler.py:135,:247) are completely unreferenced by outline_agent.py. verified_compute (redesign doc T1 step 5, doc :393-397) does not exist anywhere in src/. So every computed number terminates at the planner forever — the ONLY legitimate render lane is unreachable, and the [#calc:] invariant is unfalsifiable because nothing can ever exercise it.
findings.evidence: grep for verified_compute across src/ returns zero hits outside docs; grep for calc_token/execute_quantified_model shows callers only inside generator/, none in outline/; outline_agent.py:1620 bar_reason string promises 'may render only via the verified [#calc:]/ModelSpec lane' — a lane the agent cannot invoke.
findings.fix: Implement verified_compute in the new src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py per doc T1 step 5: tool takes named inputs each bound to an evidence span (ev_id + raw_literal + literal_start/end resolved from workspace.ev_store) plus a formula; builds a ModelSpec with SourcedInputs, calls execute_quantified_model (fail-closed on non-executing model), returns a ToolResult whose markdown carries display_value + the [#calc:model_id:spec_hash:field_id] token and NEVER a [CITE:ev_xxx] for the derived number; register it in OutlineAgent._build_registry.
findings.acceptance: New unit test: verified_compute over fixture evidence rows returns success ToolResult whose markdown contains '[#calc:' and no '[CITE:' for the derived value, with a persisted QuantifiedResult; a companion assert shows execute_python stats still land renderable=False. registry.get_tool('verified_compute') is not None after _build_registry().

findings.problem: outline_toolkit.py does not exist and ALL nine non-compute T1 tools are missing. _build_registry() = build_default_registry() (the 8 analysis tools, tool_registry.py:690) + inspect_basket + search_more_evidence + update_outline (+ finish_outline pseudo-action). Missing: fetch_url, search_corpus, get_evidence, list_baskets, calculator, coverage_audit, preview_section_evidence, find_contradictions, corroboration_profile. Concrete consequences on a 346-basket run: the agent cannot read the FULL text of any ev_id (get_evidence), cannot keyword/BM25-search the folded ev_store (search_corpus), cannot enumerate baskets (list_baskets — inspect_basket at :873 requires already knowing a basket_id), has no deterministic never-LLM coverage accounting (coverage_audit) so section-coverage judgments ride entirely on the LLM gap checklist, and has no arithmetic primitive (calculator) despite the whitelisted-AST evaluator already existing at tradeoff_modeler.py:135/:247. find_contradictions exists only as the cross-run memory hook (memory/verified_claim_graph.py:229), not the per-basket corpus tool the doc specifies (doc :102).
findings.evidence: grep -rn 'fetch_url|search_corpus|list_baskets|coverage_audit|preview_section_evidence|corroboration_profile|verified_compute' src/ --include='*.py' hits only unrelated code (utils/ingest.py:2110 fetch_url helper, frame_fetcher._fetch_url_pattern:1187, verified_claim_graph:229); no register_outline_toolkit, no src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py.
findings.fix: Create src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py with register_outline_toolkit(registry, workspace, agent_model, deadline) implementing the nine tools per doc :377-383 (calculator wraps tradeoff_modeler's AST evaluator; coverage_audit is pure-Python accounting over workspace.baskets/outline_draft; get_evidence/search_corpus/list_baskets read workspace.ev_store/baskets); call it at the end of OutlineAgent._build_registry().
findings.acceptance: After OutlineAgent._build_registry(), set(registry names) is a superset of {fetch_url, search_corpus, get_evidence, list_baskets, calculator, coverage_audit, preview_section_evidence, find_contradictions, corroboration_profile, verified_compute}; pytest tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_toolkit.py passes including calculator AST-rejection cases (e.g. '__import__("os")' rejected) and the fold-in id-collision assert.

findings.problem: The fold-in seam is not factored as a reusable unit, so fetch_url (doc :32, :380-382 — 'fetch_url MUST reuse _offset_renumber + _stamp_and_delete') cannot be built without creating a SECOND seam. _offset_renumber (:620) and _stamp_and_delete (:802) are module-private helpers, but the actual fold sequence — existing_urls dedup (:757-763), next_ev_offset (:765), _offset_renumber (:767), _stamp_and_delete (:769-771), the ev_store insert loop (:772-773), and the disclosure (:774-782) — is inlined inside _tool_search_more_evidence. A fetch_url implementation would have to copy-paste that block; any later drift between the two copies (e.g. one gains a new screen, the other doesn't) lets externally fetched rows enter ev_store unstamped or un-renumbered, violating the binding invariant that external content re-enters ONLY via the one fold-in seam.
findings.evidence: outline_agent.py:772 'for row in kept_rows: workspace.ev_store[row["evidence_id"]] = row' is the only external-row write path and it lives inside _tool_search_more_evidence; nothing exports the composed sequence.
findings.fix: Extract a single shared async fold helper (doc suggests outline/_fold_in.py or outline_toolkit.py): fold_rows_into_workspace(workspace, rows, research_question, agent_model, label) performing dedup -> offset -> _offset_renumber -> _stamp_and_delete -> ev_store insert -> disclose, re-point _tool_search_more_evidence to it, then implement fetch_url as single-URL fetch (reuse src/utils/ingest.fetch_url:2110 or frame_fetcher._fetch_url_pattern:1187) -> row shaping -> the SAME fold call.
findings.acceptance: grep shows exactly one code path writing external rows into workspace.ev_store (the shared fold helper); a test asserts a fetch_url'd row receives an offset-renumbered ev_id, passes the stamp screen, appears in workspace.disclosures, and that an injected id collision raises AssertionError (the :625 fail-loud seam).

findings.problem: ToolDefinition has only name/description/requires_data/requires_llm/parameters/execute — no tags/core fields, which doc T1 step 1 (:385) requires so the decide prompt can list core tools in full and group the rest (doc :156-158). Once the ~10 T1 tools land, the flat available_tools() listing in the decide prompt (outline_agent.py:1163-1165) grows unbounded and unclassified, bloating every decide turn on GLM-5.2 and burying the outline verbs among analysis tools.
findings.evidence: tool_registry.py:42-50 dataclass fields; outline_agent.py:1163 'available = self.registry.available_tools(True) + [_FINISH_ACTION]' renders one flat list.
findings.fix: Add tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list) and core: bool = True to ToolDefinition (defaults keep the existing 12 registrations byte-compatible); set tags/core in register_outline_toolkit; render the decide prompt as core-tools-in-full + one grouped line per tag family.
findings.acceptance: ToolDefinition(name='x', description='d', requires_data=False, requires_llm=False, tags=['corpus'], core=False) constructs; a decide-prompt unit test asserts core tools appear with full descriptions and non-core tools appear grouped; existing 14 outline tests still pass.

findings.problem: Edge in the new digest (the P1 surfacing fix): in _result_digest_lines, when the FIRST stats pair alone exceeds the budget, the guard 'len(...) > budget and shown' is falsy (shown is empty) so the pair is appended anyway; the for-else then calls _emit('stats: ...') which fails the budget check and drops the ENTIRE stats line silently — no '+N omitted' disclosure. Related: when pair 1 overflows with more pairs following, the emitted '(+N more omitted)' line itself contains the oversized pair and exceeds the budget. This recreates, for that step, exactly the undisclosed-unreachable-result failure the digest exists to end.
findings.evidence: Trace with statistics={'table': '<700-char string>'} and PG_NOTEBOOK_SUMMARY_RESULT_CHARS=600: pairs=[p1]; i=0, shown=[] -> guard falsy -> shown=[p1]; else-branch _emit returns False; out has no stats line and no omission marker.
findings.fix: Before the loop, handle the single-oversized-pair case: truncate the value to fit and disclose, e.g. emit f'stats: {k}={str(v)[:budget-len(k)-20]}… (truncated to budget)'; also cap the '(+N more omitted)' line by truncating shown pairs to the budget.
findings.acceptance: New unit test: a ToolResult with one 700-char stat value and budget 600 produces a digest line containing the stat key and a truncation marker (not an empty stats section); existing digest tests stay green.
